mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5 percent, consumer spending expectations collapse and equity valuations contract, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals broad-based economic recovery, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered quarters or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and corporate earnings growth, yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, liquidity injections and yield curve steepening trigger short-term equity rallies within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows or fails to outperform single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units signal renewed consumer confidence and construction sector demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month during housing stabilization.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in over 65 percent of months where HOUST surpasses 600 thousand units during the stabilization regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all 5-day forward return windows or the signal shows no predictive accuracy in out-of-sample backtests.
